page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24 is a compute-focused instance featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. Operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server includes 90.0 GB of system memory, yielding 3.75 GB per core. The processor features a 260 MB L3 cache, alongside 48 KB L1d and 2 MB L2 caches. It lacks local NVMe storage and does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data shows strong single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core scores reaching 1912.0, placing it in the top 25% tier. This strength is also reflected in image processing and text processing benchmarks, which both rank in the top 25%. Multi-core performance is generally average, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 13690.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 34968.15. Memory bandwidth is measured at 1681.44 GB/sec for cached small blocks and drops to 139.38 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. These hardware features contribute to top-tier text generation speeds of 396.71 tokens/sec for small 135M LLM models, though performance scales down to an average 17.23 tokens/sec for medium 7B models. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which represents a key architectural tradeoff for disk-heavy operations.

This configuration is suited for CPU-bound tasks that benefit from vector and matrix acceleration, such as software compilation, static web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference. It also supports general-purpose workloads like database operations and Redis caching, where its balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio provides stable, mid-tier throughput.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24 is an x86_64 server configured with 24 dedicated vCPUs and 90.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. Featuring 12 physical cores with hyper-threading, the processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core, image processing, and text processing scores ranking in the top 25%. It also achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 396.71 tokens/sec for small LLM models. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. This server is suited for compute-intensive workloads, software compilation, static web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24 features 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 90 GB RAM, and advanced AMX/AVX-512 instructions, delivering strong single-core performance for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24 is a 24-vCPU, 90 GB RAM server with an Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for compute-intensive workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 24-vCPU compute instance featuring Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for accelerated vector and inference workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated vCPUs and 90 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix calculations.
Strong single-core performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks.
Top-tier text generation speeds for small-scale language model inference.
No local storage or GPU resources included in the base configuration.
Suited for software compilation, static web serving, and database operations.
